items.title: Binary classification output layer
items.description: Neural networks for spam, churn, or defect detection often end with one linear unit plus sigmoid to produce P(y=1|x) in (0, 1), paired with binary cross-entropy during training.

items.title: LSTM and GRU gating
items.description: Recurrent architectures use sigmoid on gate pre-activations so forget, input, and output gates stay in (0, 1) and can multiply cell state selectively.

items.title: Attention and mixture weights (historical)
items.description: Older attention mechanisms and mixture-of-experts routing sometimes applied sigmoid or softmax to produce bounded weights; modern transformers rely more on softmax for normalized attention scores.

items.title: Probability calibration layers
items.description: When a model must emit a calibrated probability rather than an unbounded score, a final sigmoid (or softmax for multi-class) converts logits to valid mass in (0, 1).

items.title: Teaching and baseline MLPs
items.description: Shallow educational networks and historical perceptron demos use sigmoid hidden units to illustrate activation, saturation, and why depth exposed the vanishing-gradient problem.
